summary: A radio station website called 'The Country Channel' with a public-facing site and an admin dashboard to manage shows and DJs.
architectural_notes: Show scheduling and 'On Air Now' logic must use CST (Central Standard Time / America/Chicago) timezone for all time comparisons.

existing_features.title: Build a public-facing homepage for 'The Country Channel' radio station that displays the station name/logo, a hero section with a live-stream play button (non-functional placeholder), a featured shows section, and a DJ roster section.
Create a Shows page that lists all radio shows with their name, description, schedule/time slot, and associated DJ name.
Create a DJs page that lists all DJs with their name, bio, photo URL (or placeholder), and associated shows.

Build an Admin Dashboard accessible via a protected route (/admin). The dashboard must have a sidebar with navigation to 'Manage Shows' and 'Manage DJs' sections, plus a summary overview (total shows count, total DJs count).

Implement a Shows management interface in the admin dashboard allowing the admin to create, edit, and delete shows via a form modal. The form must include fields for title, description, scheduleDay, scheduleTime, and DJ selection (dropdown populated from the DJs list).
Implement a DJs management interface in the admin dashboard allowing the admin to create, edit, and delete DJs via a form modal. The form must include fields for name, bio, and photoUrl.

Apply a cohesive visual theme throughout the site inspired by classic country music aesthetics: warm earthy tones (browns, tans, burnt oranges), bold serif/slab typography for headings, woodgrain or rustic texture accents, and a dark rich background for the admin dashboard. Enforce this theme consistently across all public and admin pages.

Add a `useCurrentShow` React Query hook in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` that calls the backend `getCurrentShow` query, polling every 60 seconds so the displayed show stays current without a page refresh.

Create an `OnAirNow` component styled with the country theme (warm earthy tones, serif typography). The component displays a pulsing red 'ON AIR' badge, the current show's title, schedule time, and the assigned DJ's name. When no show is currently scheduled, display a friendly 'Nothing on air right now — stay tuned!' message.

Embed the `OnAirNow` component prominently on the public Home page (`frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx`), placed between the hero section and the featured shows section. Also add a compact version of the 'On Air Now' indicator to the site Navigation bar (`frontend/src/components/Navigation.tsx`) so it is visible on all public pages.

Embed the Live365 iframe player (`<iframe width="450" height="316" frameborder="0" src="https://live365.com/embeds/v1/player/a57949?s=md&m=dark&c=mp3"></iframe>`) inside the `OnAirNow` component (`frontend/src/components/OnAirNow.tsx`), positioned prominently within the section. The iframe must be responsive — wrap it in a container that caps its max width and centers it horizontally so it fits well on both mobile and desktop layouts while preserving the existing country theme styling around it.

new_feature_requests.summary: Public-facing radio station website for The Country Channel
Admin dashboard to manage shows (create, edit, delete)
Admin dashboard to manage DJs (create, edit, delete)
Implement a backend data model and CRUD API for Shows. Each show must store: id, title, description, scheduleDay (e.g. Monday), scheduleTime, djId (optional reference), and a createdAt timestamp.
Implement a backend data model and CRUD API for DJs. Each DJ must store: id, name, bio, photoUrl, and a createdAt timestamp.
Add an 'On Air Now' display on the public-facing site to show which show is currently live/airing.

Add a backend function `getCurrentShow` that determines which show is currently on air by matching the current day of the week and time against each show's `scheduleDay` and `scheduleTime` fields. Return the matching Show record (with optional DJ info) or null/empty if nothing is currently scheduled.

Embed the Live365 iframe player (`<iframe width="450" height="316" frameborder="0" src="https://live365.com/embeds/v1/player/a57949?s=md&m=dark&c=mp3"></iframe>`) inside the `OnAirNow` component (`frontend/src/components/OnAirNow.tsx`), positioned prominently within the section. The iframe must be responsive — wrap it in a container that caps its max width and centers it horizontally so it fits well on both mobile and desktop layouts while preserving the existing country theme styling around it.
Ensure the `getCurrentShow` backend function uses CST (Central Standard Time) timezone when matching the current day and time against show schedules.

Update the `getCurrentShow` backend function in `backend/main.mo` to determine the current day of the week and current time using CST (Central Standard Time, UTC-6) instead of UTC. Apply the UTC-6 offset to the IC system time before matching against each show's `scheduleDay` and `scheduleTime` fields so that shows go on air at the correct local CST times.
